name: pcs-tools
description: PCS Tools internal Claude Code plugins for the Kit Builder, MAP pricing, and Jira-task-builder workflows.

plugins.name: pcs-promo-parser
plugins.description: Parse vendor promo decks (Milwaukee, DeWalt, Makita, Bosch, EGO, Flex, GearWrench, Crescent) into PCS Kit Builder Stage 1 outputs. v1.4.0: robust + selective PDF ingestion (PyMuPDF in-process render when the native rasteriser is missing — no poppler; cover/marketing pages skipped from the vision read for big token savings, no accuracy loss); the Milwaukee SKU pattern now admits sub-brand forms (MX Fuel, letter-infix, bare, Empire, Stiletto, apparel, layout) so legit SKUs stop being held to For-Review; ambiguous split battery slides ask the operator to confirm pairing before holding. v1.3.0: a per-SKU + per-price verification gate independently re-grounds every SKU/price in the deck before writing (deterministic text grep + an adversarial verifier subagent + a vendor-regex check) and HOLDS anything ungroundable out of the Promo-List (For-Review `unverified`) — hallucinated SKUs/prices can't reach NetSuite. v1.2.0: new `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<session>/Promo Parsed Output/` folder tree; BMSM, e-rebate, and promo-code promos are parsed into a combined Other-Promotions.csv instead of excluded; a conditional For-Review.xlsx flags low-confidence + missing-data pages; empty outputs aren't written (Parser-Audit.csv is the always-present manifest).

plugins.name: pcs-map-updater
plugins.description: Merge a vendor MAP sheet with a NetSuite ERP export, flag exceptions, and export a review-ready Excel workbook for the monthly MAP update workflow.

plugins.name: pcs-jira-task-builder
plugins.description: Beta. Reads pcs-promo-parser Stage 1 CSVs (Promo Parsed Output/ folder) and creates matching Jira Tasks in the PCS Promotions space. Hard PROM write-protection (literal-phrase gate), defaults to PAT, per-vendor period naming, auto-HERO detection, RSA per-row review, storefront link scaffolding. v0.2.0: also creates Tasks from the parser's Other-Promotions.csv (BMSM / e-rebate / promo-code) with per-group review and BMSM/E-Rebate/Coupon labels; those three are no longer Non-Included reasons; tolerates absent empty files via the Parser-Audit manifest.

plugins.name: pcs-promo-creation
plugins.description: Guided end-to-end promo pipeline: parse a vendor deck -> build NetSuite kit imports -> create Jira promotion tasks, with a Yes/No confirmation before each stage and the hard PROM write-protection at the Jira step. Surfaces a drag-and-drop file-upload artifact at every document step (deck, cheat sheet, NetSuite export). An AI validation pass scans each stage's imports/exports before its gate, auto-correcting safe issues and flagging the rest. v1.13.0: when the NS export shows SKUs not yet built in NetSuite, the kit stage captures a resume kit — the unbuilt-SKU list, an unbuilt DECODE (copy-paste artifact), and a separate Unbuilt-SKU Promo-List of just the affected kits, plus a README — saved to the session folder so a later/separate chat can pick those kits up once the items are built. v1.12.0: the NetSuite kit stage surfaces the Promo Kit Support saved-search link (account 855722, search 16021) in both the Step 3 chat text and the DECODE copy-paste artifact, so the operator clicks straight through to run the search. v1.11.0: token discovery is robust — it finds the GitHub token in a subfolder or oddly-named file and extracts github_pat_/ghp_ by shape instead of assuming a GITHUB_TOKEN= line, and the non-Windows (Cowork Linux sandbox) source install is fixed (token-injected pip URL, drop winget). v1.10.0: Step 0 settles the Kit Builder up front — if `kb.exe` is missing and a `.env` token is present it installs it before parsing (no longer deferred to the kit stage); if no token is found it tells the operator to get the GitHub token `.env` from their admin and offers to continue without kit building (deck parsing + Jira still run; the NetSuite kit stages are skipped). v1.9.0: the Kit Builder ships as a prebuilt headless `kb.exe` — Step 0 checks `kb.exe --version` (>= 0.5.22) and on Windows Claude fetches it from the private Release via the `.env` token (no Python/pip/Git; source pip install stays as the dev/non-Windows fallback). v1.8.0: Stage-1 validation enforces the parser's verification gate — re-matches every emitted SKU to the vendor regex, asserts no held SKU leaked into an output, reconciles SKUs Held, and surfaces verification-held items at Gate 2. v1.7.0: requires kb >= 0.5.21 (collapses duplicate kit members sharing an Item ID); the Step 0 prereq check uses `pip show pcs-kit-builder-lite` (no `kb --version`). v1.6.0: the DECODE formula is shown as a copy-paste artifact (one Copy button per block) in chat. v1.5.0: a run lands in one session folder `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<session>/` with Promo Parsed Output / NetSuite Import Files / Images subfolders; the upload widget is recreated on every file (re)prompt; the parser's For-Review workbook is surfaced at the kit gate. v1.4.0: kit images are composed locally via a `kb --images-only` one-liner (macOS + Windows) since Cowork's sandbox can't reach NetSuite's image host. Orchestrates pcs-promo-parser and pcs-jira-task-builder and drives the Kit Builder `kb.exe` CLI binary (>= 0.5.22); Claude writes each kit's Page Title and Detailed Description from the item data using the ported title/description rules; prompts for deck, pricing cheat sheet, and NetSuite Promo Kit Support export uploads when needed.
